goal: Add the Bluepilot side of the shared one-shot write permit contentHash canon: a pure, side-effect-free canonicalization and SHA-256 helper with the fixed cross-repo test vector. This is stage 1 only: no permit register, no corridor change, no SmartPush/write-path change, no runtime flag change, and no live write.
scope_out: Do not implement a permit register, consume operation, expiry, policy, or corridor check.
Do not modify SmartPush, the Maya gate client, the sandbox real-write endpoint, target profiles, providers, or server routes.
Do not add dependencies or change package scripts.
Do not perform a live GitHub write or call a live Maya endpoint.
Do not introduce new Opus-branded names for this new helper.
claims: builder/src/writePermitContentHash.ts exports a pure canonical byte builder and computeWritePermitContentHash helper.
The canonical byte form length-prefixes repo, branch, path, op, baseSha, and content in that exact order with 8-digit byte lengths and a colon.
The helper hashes raw canonical bytes with SHA-256 and returns lowercase hex prefixed by sha256:.
The fixed shared vector G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox main probe/hash-vector.txt create empty-baseSha hello-LF yields sha256:4cf2991e34c573d82852a5293d9fae147c0bec2249fa9c71cae75b8ef7728576.
Tests prove the fixed vector, byte sensitivity, and coordinate binding without network, DB, token, or write side effects.
assumptions: The same contentHash canon is implemented in maya-core in a paired branch for BP-144 stage 1.
The downloaded BP-144 stage-1 spec is the user-approved source, but Codex verifies the vector independently before implementing.
Stage 2 and 3 will wire permits through maya-core and Bluepilot later; this task deliberately does not.
dod: builder/src/writePermitContentHash.ts exists and has no runtime side effects.
builder/tests/writePermitContentHash.test.ts passes and asserts the fixed vector exactly.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
No forbidden write-path, corridor, provider, package, or tool files changed.
stop_conditions: The fixed vector cannot be reproduced exactly.
A permit register, corridor change, SmartPush change, or live write becomes necessary.
A package or dependency change becomes necessary.
The paired maya-core implementation cannot use the same canon.
prior_task_findings: BP-143 documented the one-shot permit design. Claude's BP-144 stage-1 spec was corrected against local paths and independently verified: the test vector hash is sha256:4cf2991e34c573d82852a5293d9fae147c0bec2249fa9c71cae75b8ef7728576. This task implements only the shared contentHash canon in Bluepilot; the maya-core side is built in a paired branch under maya-core local rules.
